title: Bard — Per-Device Identity Enrollment (Sprint B2 / ADR-0010, pragmatic JWT-class)
description: FROZEN 2026-06-12. Extended 2026-06-12 (Sprint B6, additive only): optional DeviceRecord.workgroup (console-assigned grouping, set via POST /devices/{id}/workgroup; rename uses the existing label property via POST /devices/{id}/rename). The v2 "software identity keys + JWT-class auth" enrollment lifecycle: each device is issued its OWN signing key (per-device HMAC secret for v2) through a join-token -> pending -> active -> revoked state machine. This is NOT the v3 hybrid-PQ/MLS trust fabric (control-plane.openapi.yaml DRAFT): no ML-DSA, no MLS KeyPackages, no hardware attestation here. The verifier seam (PerDeviceVerifier resolving the key by deviceId) is the slot where asymmetric / PQ credentials replace the per-device HMAC later without touching call sites. Per-device tokens carry sub=deviceId. The per-device secret is server-held and MUST NOT appear in any response payload defined here.

DeviceId.description: Stable identifier for an enrolled device; equals the JWT 'sub' on per-device tokens.

DeviceState.description: Enrollment lifecycle state. join-token -> (enroll) -> pending -> (approve) -> active -> (revoke) -> revoked. revoked and unknown both fail verification.

JoinToken.description: Short-lived bearer issued by the registry manager out-of-band to a device operator. Presented once at /enroll to create a pending device record. A join token is NOT a device credential and cannot be used to mint per-device tokens.

DeviceRecord.description: The authoritative per-device record. The per-device signing secret is held server-side and is deliberately absent from this shape.

label.description: Operator-supplied human label (a writer-facing name, not a technical id).

approvedAt.description: When a manager approved the device (-> active).

workgroup.description: Sprint B6 (additive): console-assigned workgroup for fleet grouping. Absent or null = not in a group. v2 derives workgroupId deterministically from the name (wg_ + base64url(sha256(name)) truncated to 22 chars, matching trust.schema.yaml WorkgroupId); v3's MLS-backed workgroups replace the derivation behind the same shape.

EnrollRequest.description: POST /enroll body: present a valid join token to register this device as pending.

EnrollResponse.description: POST /enroll 200: the newly-pending device record. No secret is returned; the device receives its per-device secret only after manager approval, out of band of this contract.

ApproveResponse.description: POST /devices/{id}/approve 200: device transitions pending -> active. The freshly-minted per-device HMAC secret is returned EXACTLY ONCE to the approving manager to hand to the device operator; it is never persisted in or recoverable from DeviceRecord, and never re-emitted.

deviceSecret.description: Per-device HMAC signing secret (v2). One-time disclosure. The verifier seam permits an asymmetric public key here instead in v3.
